D6 Display L5 Evaluate Reference

Light → Melatonin Suppression

the ipRGC clock signal rises with melanopic light along an S-curve — modest at screen levels, saturating under daylight

ipRGCs report melanopic illuminance to the circadian clock, suppressing night-time melatonin. The dose–response is sigmoidal: little effect at very low light, a steep middle, and saturation when bright. The curve below is illustrative — the exact half-suppression point depends heavily on exposure duration and a person's prior light history.

Slide the melanopic illuminance and read the suppression. Markers show typical levels: an evening phone, an indoor room, an overcast sky, full daylight. Switch the exposure duration — longer light lowers the threshold, so the same screen bites a little harder over a long evening.
